sections.paragraph: f., the skull, so called from its round form (root גָּלַל), 2Ki 9:35. This word is also used where the single individuals of a nation are numbered; as in. Lat. caput, Germ. Kopf [Engl. heads]; Exo 16:16, עֹמֶר לַגֻּלְגֹּלֶת “an omer apiece;” Num 1:2, כָּל־זָכָר לְגֻלְגְּלֹתָם “all the males according to their heads,” i.e. man by man, verses 18 , 20 , 22 compare רֹאשׁ Jud. 5:30 . (In the Rabb. כֶּסֶף הַגֻּלְגֹּלֶת signifies a poll-tax; Syr. ܓܓܳܘܽܠܬܐܳ id. by casting away Lamed of the first syllable; Arabic جَلَجَةُ, where the second Lamed is cast away; comp. Γολγοθᾶ Matth. 27:33 .)
